issues.claim: engineering layer's operational scoping of Sanskrit terminology
issues.objection: The engineering rendering of BG 2.54 narrows the Sanskrit's scope (Arjuna asks for description) to a specifically operational frame. Acceptable simplification — the source pack preserves the fuller commentarial scope — but a careful Sanskritist notes the narrowing.

notes: The engineering layer for BG 2.54 preserves the verse's structural shape: Arjuna asks for description; engineering layer preserves the recognition-question structure (per Shankara's gloss — bhāṣā = description by which others identify). engineering layer respects Ramanuja's nature-inferred-from-conduct frame. lakṣaṇa/definition-inquiry preserved. No tradition is misattributed, no doctrinally load-bearing word is dropped without acknowledgment. The Sanskrit terms (sthita-prajña / sthita-dhīḥ / samādhi-sthasya / kūrma / rasa / param) appear preserved verbatim where invoked. PASS.
